title: Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Cognizant Technology Solutions compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Operating Cash Flow of roughly $2.88B minus $288M in Capex yields a robust $2.59B in Free Cash Flow. A conservative 3% growth rate acknowledges the secular headwinds in traditional IT outsourcing and the macro-driven slowdown in discretionary tech spending, partially offset by strength in healthcare and digital modernization.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9% discount rate reflects the execution risks associated with pivoting a massive legacy services business toward high-margin AI and cloud consulting. The threat of generative AI disintermediating routine coding tasks justifies a higher risk premium than pure-play software peers.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2% terminal rate models CTSH growing slightly below historical GDP levels. This accounts for the permanent deflationary pressures of AI on the IT services headcount model, recognizing that revenue growth will increasingly decouple from employee growth.

thesisTitle: A Value Trap or a Cash Machine in Transition?
thesisParagraphs: Cognizant operates in one of the most structurally challenged segments of the technology sector. The traditional model of massive offshore 'body shopping' for application maintenance and business process outsourcing is under intense pressure from generative AI and nimble, cloud-native consultancies. The market has severely punished CTSH, pricing in flat to negative growth as enterprise clients pull back on discretionary spending. Consequently, its valuation multiple has compressed significantly relative to its historical averages and broader tech peers.

However, the current valuation ignores the sheer cash-generating power of the business. Even in a low-growth scenario, Cognizant's deep embeddedness within Fortune 500 infrastructure creates immense switching costs. This translates to incredibly sticky, recurring revenue and over $2.5 billion in annual free cash flow. Management is astutely deploying this cash into aggressive share repurchases and tuck-in acquisitions to accelerate its pivot toward digital engineering. While top-line growth may remain sluggish, the combination of a compressed multiple, high free cash flow yield, and shrinking share count presents a compelling margin of safety.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via SEC and Finviz (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a low 3% growth rate for Cognizant?
faq.answer: Gemini models conservative top-line growth due to significant macro headwinds and intense competition in the IT services sector. Many enterprises have delayed large digital transformation projects, directly impacting CTSH's core revenue streams.

faq.question: Is generative AI a threat to Cognizant's business?
faq.answer: Yes, it is a significant structural threat. Generative AI can automate much of the routine coding, testing, and process work that historically required massive offshore teams. CTSH must successfully pivot to higher-level, AI-driven consulting, or its legacy business will face severe deflation.

faq.question: Why does the model show Cognizant as undervalued?
faq.answer: Despite sluggish growth prospects, the market has heavily discounted CTSH. The company generates massive amounts of free cash flow with very low capital intensity. Because it is priced so cheaply, even minimal growth, combined with aggressive share buybacks, makes it mathematically undervalued in a DCF model.
